message:
perf(web): add Lighthouse mobile baseline report

Capture baseline performance metrics for priority pages:
- Home: 68 performance, 95 accessibility
- Transcript: 54 performance (needs work), 100 accessibility
- Distillations: 67 performance, 96 accessibility
- Method: 69 performance, 96 accessibility

Key findings:
- CLS = 0 across all pages (excellent)
- LCP 6.7-9.0s is the main bottleneck (poor)
- TBT 360-670ms needs improvement
- SEO and Best Practices at 100

See mobile-baseline-2025-12-30.md for recommendations.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.5","title":"install.sh: brenner binary install path (asset selection + checksum + atomic install)","description":"# Goal
Implement the **`brenner` fast-path** inside `install.sh`:
- resolve the correct release asset for OS/arch
- verify checksum
- install to the destination safely

---

# Reference patterns to emulate
- `cm` release: per-binary assets + `.sha256` files
- `cm` installer: checksum verification with `sha256sum` (Linux) or `shasum -a 256` (macOS)
- `cass` installer: supports `--artifact-url` and `--checksum` overrides for CI / offline installs

---

# Requirements
- Must not require Bun to install the compiled `brenner` binary.
- Must support CI offline installs via:
  - `--artifact-url file://...`
  - `--checksum ...`
- Must support safe installation:
  - download to temp file
  - verify checksum
  - final `install -m 0755` or atomic move into destination

---

# Acceptance criteria
- On Linux + macOS, `install.sh --easy-mode --verify` installs `brenner` correctly.
- `--verify` step can execute `brenner --version` or `brenner help` successfully.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.6","title":"install.sh: install ntm/cass/cm from pinned versions + verify","description":"# Goal
Inside `install.sh`, install the **external operator toolchain** and verify it:
- `ntm`
- `cass`
- `cm`

---

# Key constraint
We must **pin versions by tag** (via the toolchain manifest) and avoid fetching mutable `main` installers whenever a pinned tag exists.

---

# Upstream patterns to emulate
- `cass` / `cm` one-liners:
  - `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify`
- `ntm` one-liner:
  - `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash`

---

# Design notes
## Installation approach
For each tool, choose one:
1) **Direct binary download + checksum verification** (preferred)
2) **Call upstream installer script at pinned tag** (fallback)

`ntm` is special:
- Unix-only (tmux)
- installer script does not appear to verify checksums and its `--version` behavior should be audited

## Verification approach
We want deterministic commands with meaningful exit codes:
- `ntm deps -v` (Unix-only)
- `cass health`
- `cm --version` (optionally `cm onboard status`)

---

# Acceptance criteria
- `install.sh` installs `ntm/cass/cm` by pinned version.
- `--verify` executes the verification commands and fails if any required tool is missing/broken.
- Supports `--skip-ntm`, `--skip-cass`, `--skip-cm` flags.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:41:39.050474905-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:44:36.979717283-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:44:36.979717283-05:00","close_reason":"install.sh installs ntm/cass/cm via pinned toolchain manifest (tag URLs), supports --skip-* flags, and verifies via ntm deps -v / cass health / cm --version; fixed manifest URLs+versions + spec.","labels":["installer","security","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:41:39.071174512-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.7","title":"install.sh: finalize UX contract (flags, logging, easy-mode PATH, dry-run)","description":"# Goal
Make `install.sh` behave like a first-class operator tool:
- predictable flags
- minimal, structured output
- safe defaults

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` / `cm` installers support:
  - `--easy-mode` (non-interactive)
  - `--verify`
  - `--dest` + `--system`
  - `--artifact-url` / `--checksum` overrides
  - `--quiet`

We should match these wherever possible so the mental model transfers.

---

# Proposed flag set
- `--version vX.Y.Z` (brenner version)
- `--toolchain <path or url>` (defaults to repo manifest)
- `--dest DIR` and/or `--system`
- `--easy-mode`
- `--verify`
- `--quiet`
- `--dry-run` (print install plan + exit)
- `--skip-ntm` / `--skip-cass` / `--skip-cm`

---

# Acceptance criteria
- `install.sh --help` documents all flags.
- `--easy-mode` runs without prompts and ensures PATH guidance is actionable.
- `--dry-run` prints a deterministic plan derived from the toolchain manifest.
- Output is minimal by default (no spam), but failures are verbose/actionable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:41:49.834127198-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:18:56.692245487-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:18:56.692245487-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in install.sh: --easy-mode (PATH config), --dry-run (preview), --verbose (debug), --verify (post-install check), --skip-brenner. Structured logging with log_info/error/warn/debug.","labels":["installer","ops","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:41:49.864227083-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.8","title":"install.ps1: brenner Windows install path (asset selection + checksum + PATH)","description":"# Goal
Implement the Windows install surface for `brenner` (PowerShell):
- resolve the correct Windows release asset
- verify SHA256 checksum
- install into a user-writable bin dir and optionally add to PATH

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` uses `install.ps1 -EasyMode -Verify` and verifies checksum with `Get-FileHash`.

---

# Requirements
- Must not require Bun.
- Must support `-ArtifactUrl` + `-Checksum` overrides (for CI `file://` installs).
- Must support `-EasyMode` for non-interactive install.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- `install.ps1 -EasyMode -Verify` installs `brenner.exe` and can execute `brenner.exe --help`.
- Adds destination to user PATH in `-EasyMode` (or prints exact instructions otherwise).

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.9","title":"install.ps1: install cass/cm (skip ntm) + verification","description":"# Goal
Extend `install.ps1` to install and verify the Windows-supported external tools:
- `cass`
- `cm`

and explicitly skip/warn for `ntm` (tmux / Unix).

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` installer already provides a PowerShell install script.
- `cm` is Bun-compiled and should be installable as a single `.exe` plus `.sha256`.

---

# Requirements
- Pinned versions (from the toolchain manifest).
- Checksum verification for downloaded binaries.
- `-Verify` runs:
  - `cass.exe --version` or `cass.exe health`
  - `cm.exe --version`

---

# Acceptance criteria
- `install.ps1 -EasyMode -Verify` installs `cass` + `cm` and confirms they run.
- If `ntm` is requested on Windows, script prints a clear warning and continues.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3","title":"CLI: brenner doctor + upgrade (toolchain verification)","description":"# Goal
Make the system **self-diagnosing**.

Add a `brenner doctor` command that can be used by:
- humans (copy/paste guidance)
- installers (non-interactive `--json` mode)
- CI (smoke checks)

Also add a `brenner upgrade` command (optional) to reduce the need for users to remember installer URLs.

# What `brenner doctor` should check
- `brenner` version (compiled binary)
- `ntm` present and runnable (`ntm --version` and/or `ntm deps -v`)
- `cass` present (`cass --version` or `cass health`)
- `cm` present (`cm --version` and optionally `cm onboard status`)
- Agent Mail reachability when configured (`AGENT_MAIL_BASE_URL` health endpoint)

# Output
- Human-friendly summary with ✅/⚠️/✗ statuses
- `--json` for automation
- Suggestions that point to the canonical `install.sh` invocation and flags (e.g., `--skip-ntm` on Windows)

# Acceptance criteria
- `brenner doctor` exists and runs without network.
- It fails with a non-zero exit code when required tools are missing (configurable via flags).
- `--json` output is stable enough for CI parsing.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:36:35.658830813-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:36:35.658830813-05:00","labels":["cass","cli","cm","installer","ntm","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:36:35.679828755-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.1","title":"Add brenner version metadata (for binaries + doctor)","description":"# Goal
Make `brenner` report a stable version.

# Why
- Installers need to verify the installed binary.
- CI needs to assert correct artifact version.
- Users need to debug “what version am I running?”

# Requirements
- `brenner --version` prints semver tag when available.
- Include build metadata:
  - git SHA
  - build date
  - platform target

# Acceptance criteria
- `brenner --version` works in both:
  - `bun ./brenner.ts ...`
  - compiled binary (`bun build --compile`)
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:38:35.444924237-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:17:15.882650334-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:17:15.882650334-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented brenner --version/version output (version, git SHA, build date, target) with env overrides; added CLI tests + README command map.","labels":["cli","installer","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:38:35.474615365-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.2","title":"Implement brenner doctor (human + --json)","description":"# Goal
Implement `brenner doctor` with both:
- human-readable output
- `--json` output for automation

# Checks (minimum)
- `brenner` version/build metadata
- presence of `ntm`, `cass`, `cm`
- optional: Agent Mail reachability (if env configured)

# Output contract
- deterministic JSON keys
- exit codes:
  - 0 = healthy (or only warnings)
  - 1 = missing required tool

# Acceptance criteria
- `brenner doctor` can be used as the installer `--verify` step.
- Runs without network (unless Agent Mail check enabled).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:38:43.272515467-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:32:45.819542653-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:32:45.819542653-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented brenner doctor (human + --json) with tool presence/verify checks + optional agent mail readiness; added tests + README update.","labels":["ci","cli","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:38:43.292682963-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:53:33.20742995-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:53:33.239467414-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:26:42.471830936-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:26:42.471830936-05:00","labels":["cli","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:26:42.472788789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.3","title":"Define release artifact matrix + naming + checksum layout","description":"# Goal
Define the **canonical release artifact contract** for `brenner`:
- which targets we build
- how files are named
- where/how checksums are published

This contract is the bridge between:
- the release workflow (producer)
- `install.sh` / `install.ps1` (consumer)
- `brenner doctor` (verification)

# Target choices (decision points)
- Linux x64: `bun-linux-x64-baseline` vs `modern` (baseline is safer).
- Whether we ship a separate musl binary (`bun-linux-x64-musl`) or keep glibc-only for v0.
- macOS: arm64 + x64.
- Windows: x64.

# Naming scheme (proposal)
Make it deterministic and grep-able:
- `brenner-${os}-${arch}` (optionally `-baseline` / `-musl` suffixes)
- Checksums: either `brenner-${...}.sha256` per artifact **or** a single `SHA256SUMS` file.

# Acceptance criteria
- A written mapping exists (table is fine) that lists:
  - supported (os, arch) tuples
  - bun compile `--target` values
  - resulting artifact filenames
  - checksum filename conventions
- The mapping is referenced by `brenner_bot-5so.11.2.1` (install.sh) and the toolchain manifest.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:41.43323114-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:52:52.506398419-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:52:52.506398419-05:00","close_reason":"Defined release artifact matrix: targets, naming scheme, checksum format, URL templates. Updated toolchain manifest with linux-arm64.","labels":["build","installer","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:41.443668983-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.4","title":"Implement GitHub Actions release workflow (binaries + checksums)","description":"# Goal
Add a **tag-driven release workflow** that builds `brenner` binaries and publishes them as GitHub release assets.

# Why
This enables the installer fast-path:
- `install.sh` can download a pinned `vX.Y.Z` artifact
- the download is verifiable via checksum

# Requirements
- Trigger on tags like `v*`.
- Build the target matrix from `brenner_bot-5so.5.5.3`.
- Compile using `bun build --compile --target=... --outfile ... ./brenner.ts`.
- Generate SHA256 checksums for every artifact and upload alongside.

# Optional (nice-to-have)
- Attach `install.sh` / `install.ps1` (and their `.sha256`) to the release so users can fetch installers by tag.
- `workflow_dispatch` dry-run mode that builds artifacts without publishing.

# Acceptance criteria
- A tag build produces:
  - binaries
  - checksum files
  - a GitHub release containing those assets
- The release artifact names match the installer mapping.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:49.880917328-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:49.880917328-05:00","labels":["build","github-actions","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:49.910028745-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:52:55.223724862-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:13.479930685-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.5","title":"Release: publish installers + checksums as release assets","description":"# Goal
Make releases self-contained for bootstrapping:
- tag releases publish **binaries + checksums** (already in scope)
- and also publish **installers + installer checksums**

This enables a safer pinned install flow:
- `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/<repo>/<tag>/install.sh | bash -s -- ...`
- optionally verify `install.sh` against `install.sh.sha256` from the release

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` dist workflow copies `install.sh` and `install.ps1` into `dist/` and attaches them to the GitHub release.
- `cm` release workflow uploads `install.sh.sha256` alongside binaries.

---

# Design notes
Publishing installer checksums doesn’t make `curl|bash` perfect, but it:
- makes audits easier
- allows CI/offline consumers to verify the exact installer script
- nudges users toward pinned-tag installs rather than `main`

---

# Acceptance criteria
- Release workflow uploads:
  - `install.sh`
  - `install.ps1` (if present)
  - `install.sh.sha256` (and optionally `install.ps1.sha256`)
- README documents a pinned-tag install option.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:04.317976662-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:04.317976662-05:00","labels":["github-actions","installer","release","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:04.328457969-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- and also publish **installers + installer checksums**

This enables a safer pinned install flow:

- optionally verify `install.sh` against `install.sh.sha256` from the release

---

# Reference patterns
- `cass` dist workflow copies `install.sh` and `install.ps1` into `dist/` and attaches them to the GitHub release.
- `cm` release workflow uploads `install.sh.sha256` alongside binaries.

---

# Design notes
Publishing installer checksums doesn’t make `curl|bash` perfect, but it:

","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:04.317976662-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:41:14.778103-05:00","labels":["github-actions","installer","release","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:43:04.328457969-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.6","title":"EPIC: Memory Integration (cass-memory / cm)","description":"# Goal
Integrate the cass memory system so sessions can:
- retrieve relevant procedural rules + past insights
- augment kickoffs with retrieved context
- (later) feed durable artifacts back into memory

---

# Why this matters
Brenner-style work compounds. Memory prevents re-deriving the same primitives and enables cumulative research.

---

# Constraints / integration stance
- **No vendor AI APIs required.** Brenner Bot should rely on `cm context`’s deterministic scoring + cass search.
- **Fail-soft**: if `cm` is missing/unconfigured, sessions still run.
- **Local-first**: memory is best-effort in local lab / cockpit workflows; do not assume it exists in hosted public web deployments.

---

# Design principle
Treat `cm` output as “memory says …” (provenance-labeled), never as transcript-grounded truth.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-67l","title":"Integrate corpusSearch into TranscriptViewer","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:14:28.543204199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:15:46.848543288-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:15:46.848543288-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated corpusSearch into TranscriptViewer with search input, results dropdown, and text highlighting"}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-69w","title":"Bug: progressive transcript loading breaks deep links/restore","description":"# Problem
With progressive mobile transcript loading (initial 8 sections + IntersectionObserver), deep links like `#section-200` and reading-position restore can fail because the target section DOM isn't rendered yet.

# Fix
- In `TranscriptViewer.tsx`, when handling URL hash and saved-position restore on mobile, ensure `mobileSectionsLoaded` is advanced to include the target section before calling `scrollIntoView`.
- Avoid repeated re-scroll loops.

# Acceptance
- Visiting `/corpus/transcript#section-200` on mobile scrolls to the correct section.
- Refresh on mobile restores reading position even when beyond the initial loaded sections.
- `apps/web` tests still pass.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:42:12.570607074-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:42:26.142616466-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-69w","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-s8x","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:42:12.593653314-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-6hj","title":"Task: Optimize mobile typography","description":"Refine typography for optimal mobile reading.

## Purpose

Typography is the foundation of reading experience. Get this wrong and everything feels off.

## Key Optimizations

### Font Sizes
- Base text: 16px minimum (no pinch-to-zoom needed)
- Headings: Proportionally scaled down for mobile
- Code blocks: Readable but compact
- Meta text: At least 12px

Current: Uses clamp() for fluid sizes. Verify values are good at 375px.

### Line Length
- Target: 45-65 characters per line
- On narrow screens, let text span full width with proper margins
- May need to adjust prose-width at mobile breakpoints

### Line Height
- Body text: 1.6-1.8 for comfortable reading
- Headings: Tighter (1.2-1.4)
- Verify current settings work on mobile

### Letter Spacing
- Verify not too tight on mobile
- Consider slight loosening for smaller sizes

### Paragraph Spacing
- Adequate whitespace between paragraphs
- Section breaks clearly visible
- Lists have proper spacing

## Files to Modify

- apps/web/src/app/globals.css
- Possibly component-specific styles

## Testing

- Read actual transcript content on 375px width
- Verify no horizontal scroll
- Compare to reference apps (Apple Notes, Notion, Medium)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text readable without zooming at 375px
- [ ] Line length feels comfortable (45-65 chars)
- [ ] Line height aids readability
- [ ] No typography-related horizontal scroll
- [ ] Looks intentional, not cramped","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:38.921493964-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:27.101944583-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:27.101944583-05:00","close_reason":"Completed as part of brenner_bot-qka: Base font bumped to 16px min (clamp(1rem, 0.95rem + 0.25vw, 1.0625rem)), xs/sm/lg sizes also adjusted for proper hierarchy. Line height already at 1.8 for prose. Typography verified at 375px.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-6hj","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-qka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:29:18.105701336-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+# Lighthouse Mobile Baseline Report
+
+**Date**: 2025-12-30
+**Agent**: OrangeSnow (claude-code, opus-4.5)
+**Bead**: brenner_bot-a1c
+
+## Test Conditions
+
+- **Form Factor**: Mobile
+- **Network**: Simulated 4G (Lighthouse default)
+- **CPU**: 4x slowdown (Lighthouse default mobile throttling)
+- **Environment**: Local dev server (http://localhost:3000)
+
+## Summary
+
+| Page | Performance | Accessibility | Best Practices | SEO |
+|------|-------------|---------------|----------------|-----|
+| Home | 68 | 95 | 100 | 100 |
+| Transcript | 54 | 100 | 100 | 100 |
+| Distillations | 67 | 96 | 100 | 100 |
+| Method | 69 | 96 | 100 | 100 |
+
+## Core Web Vitals
+
+| Page | FCP | LCP | TBT | CLS | SI |
+|------|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
+| Home | 1.1s | 6.7s | 390ms | 0 | 1.1s |
+| Transcript | 2.7s | 9.0s | 670ms | 0 | 2.7s |
+| Distillations | 1.1s | 7.0s | 380ms | 0 | 1.5s |
+| Method | 1.1s | 6.7s | 360ms | 0 | 2.3s |
+
+### Metric Thresholds (Mobile)
+
+| Metric | Good | Needs Improvement | Poor |
+|--------|------|-------------------|------|
+| FCP | ≤1.8s | 1.8-3.0s | >3.0s |
+| LCP | ≤2.5s | 2.5-4.0s | >4.0s |
+| TBT | ≤200ms | 200-600ms | >600ms |
+| CLS | ≤0.1 | 0.1-0.25 | >0.25 |
+
+## Key Findings
+
+### ✅ What's Working Well
+
+1. **Zero Layout Shift (CLS = 0)** - All pages have excellent layout stability
+2. **Perfect SEO (100)** - All pages fully optimized for search engines
+3. **Perfect Best Practices (100)** - Modern web standards fully implemented
+4. **Excellent Accessibility (95-100)** - All pages highly accessible
+
+### ⚠️ Areas Needing Improvement
+
+1. **Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)**: 6.7-9.0s
+   - All pages exceed the 4.0s "poor" threshold
+   - Root cause: Large hero images and heavy initial render
+   - Priority: **P0** - This is the primary performance bottleneck
+
+2. **Total Blocking Time (TBT)**: 360-670ms
+   - Most pages in "needs improvement" range
+   - Transcript page exceeds 600ms threshold
+   - Priority: **P1** - Affects interactivity
+
+3. **First Contentful Paint (FCP)**: 1.1-2.7s
+   - Most pages are acceptable (≤1.8s)
+   - Transcript page at 2.7s is in "needs improvement" range
+   - Priority: **P2** - Only transcript page needs attention
+
+## Worst-Performing Page
+
+**`/corpus/transcript`** (Performance: 54)
+- FCP: 2.7s (needs improvement)
+- LCP: 9.0s (poor)
+- TBT: 670ms (poor)
+- SI: 2.7s (needs improvement)
+
+This page contains 236 transcript segments - the largest content load in the app. The progressive loading implementation added in commit 9a9e8aa should help, but further optimization may be needed.
+
+## Recommendations
+
+### Immediate (P0)
+
+1. **Optimize LCP**
+   - Implement `<link rel="preload">` for hero images
+   - Consider lazy loading below-the-fold content
+   - Use `next/image` with priority for LCP elements
+   - Evaluate if initial transcript load can be reduced
+
+2. **Reduce JavaScript Bundle**
+   - Audit bundle with `@next/bundle-analyzer`
+   - Code-split heavy components (DistillationViewer, TranscriptViewer)
+   - Defer non-critical JavaScript
+
+### Short-term (P1)
+
+3. **Reduce TBT**
+   - Break up long tasks (>50ms)
+   - Use `requestIdleCallback` for non-essential work
+   - Consider web workers for heavy computation
+
+4. **Optimize Transcript Page**
+   - Implement virtualization for long lists
+   - Lazy load transcript sections on scroll
+   - Consider pagination or infinite scroll
+
+### Monitoring
+
+5. **Set up CrUX monitoring**
+   - Track real-user metrics via Chrome UX Report
+   - Set performance budgets in CI
+
+## Raw Reports
+
+JSON reports saved in this directory:
+- `home-local.json`
+- `transcript-local.json`
+- `distillations-local.json`
+- `method-local.json`
+
+## Next Steps
+
+- [ ] Implement LCP optimizations
+- [ ] Analyze bundle size
+- [ ] Add virtualization to transcript page
+- [ ] Set up CI performance budgets
+- [ ] Re-run baseline after optimizations

+      "statement": "How does the C. elegans embryo determine cell fate?",
+      "context": "C. elegans has an invariant cell lineage.",
+      "why_it_matters": "Understanding the coordinate system determines which experiments are informative.",
+      "anchors": ["§150", "§161"]
+    },
+    "hypothesis_slate": [
+      {
+        "id": "H1",
+        "name": "Lineage-based computation",
+        "claim": "Cell fate is determined by tracking lineage history.",
+        "mechanism": "Cells maintain internal state updated at division.",
+        "anchors": ["§150", "§161"]
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "H2",
+        "name": "Gradient-based computation",
+        "claim": "Cell fate is determined by reading positional signals.",
+        "mechanism": "Cells integrate morphogen concentrations.",
+        "anchors": ["§155", "§161"]
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "H3",
+        "name": "Third Alternative",
+        "claim": "Both hypotheses are wrong or the dichotomy is false.",
+        "mechanism": "Could involve epigenetic memory, mechanical forces, or stochastic commitment.",
+        "anchors": ["§103"],
+        "third_alternative": true
+      }
+    ],
+    "predictions_table": [
+      {
+        "id": "P1",
+        "condition": "Early transplant (4-8 cell stage)",
+        "predictions": { "H1": "Fate unchanged", "H2": "Fate changes", "H3": "indeterminate" }
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "P2",
+        "condition": "Late transplant (gastrulation)",
+        "predictions": { "H1": "Fate unchanged", "H2": "Fate changes", "H3": "indeterminate" }
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "P3",
+        "condition": "Ablate signalling neighbours",
+        "predictions": { "H1": "No effect", "H2": "Fate changes", "H3": "indeterminate" }
+      }
+    ],
+    "discriminative_tests": [
+      {
+        "id": "T1",
+        "name": "Stage-dependent transplantation assay",
+        "procedure": "Transplant cells at multiple developmental stages to ectopic positions.",
+        "discriminates": "H1 vs H2",
+        "expected_outcomes": { "H1": "Fate unchanged at all stages", "H2": "Fate changes at all stages" },
+        "potency_check": "Include late-stage transplants as positive control.",
+        "feasibility": "Requires C. elegans micromanipulation expertise.",
+        "score": { "likelihood_ratio": 3, "cost": 2, "speed": 2, "ambiguity": 3 }
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "T2",
+        "name": "Morphogen source ablation",
+        "procedure": "Laser-ablate cells that produce candidate morphogens.",
+        "discriminates": "H2 vs H1",
+        "expected_outcomes": { "H1": "Fates normal", "H2": "Fates disrupted" },
+        "potency_check": "Verify gradient disruption via reporter imaging.",
+        "feasibility": "Established in C. elegans labs.",
+        "score": { "likelihood_ratio": 3, "cost": 2, "speed": 2, "ambiguity": 2 }
+      }
+    ],
+    "assumption_ledger": [
+      {
+        "id": "A1",
+        "name": "Invariant lineage is reproducible",
+        "statement": "Every wild-type C. elegans embryo follows the same division pattern.",
+        "load": "If wrong, transplant results aren't generalizable.",
+        "test": "Lineage tracing in N>10 embryos.",
+        "status": "verified"
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "A2",
+        "name": "Cell identity is stable post-commitment",
+        "statement": "Once committed, fate doesn't change.",
+        "load": "If wrong, transplant assays become uninterpretable.",
+        "test": "Lineage tracing of committed cells.",
+        "status": "unchecked"
+      },
+      {
+        "id": "A3",
+        "name": "Scale check: diffusion vs cell cycle",
+        "statement": "Morphogen diffusion must be fast enough relative to cell cycle.",
+        "load": "If wrong, H2 becomes implausible.",
+        "test": "Live imaging of gradient reporters.",
+        "scale_check": true,
+        "calculation": "D ~ 10 um^2/s, L ~ 50 um => tau = L^2/D ~ 250s ~ 4 min. Cell cycle ~ 15-30 min.",
+        "implication": "Gradient-based signalling is physically plausible."
+      }
+    ],
+    "anomaly_register": [],
+    "adversarial_critique": [

+        "id": "C1",
+        "name": "The dichotomy is anachronistic",
+        "attack": "The lineage-vs-gradient distinction was formulated before modern tools.",
+        "evidence": "Single-cell trajectory analysis shows unexpected clusters.",
+        "current_status": "Moderate concern - should review recent literature.",
+        "real_third_alternative": true
+      },
+      {

+        "attack": "Cells may use chromatin states, neither lineage nor gradient.",
+        "evidence": "Epigenetic inheritance is well-documented.",
+        "current_status": "High priority for investigation."
+      }
+    ]
